title: The sound of my street in the morning
summary: Describe the sounds you hear on your street early in the morning.
topic: The sound of my street in the morning
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about the sounds on your street in the morning. Say what time you wake up, what you hear first, and what you hear later. Use present simple.
hintStarters: I live in Istanbul and every morning…
The first sound I hear is…
After six o'clock, I can hear…
sampleText: I live in Istanbul, in a small flat above a tea shop. Every morning, I wake up early because of the sounds outside. The first sound I hear is the call to prayer from the big mosque. It is very slow and beautiful. After that, I hear the seagulls. They fly over the roof and they shout at each other. At five o'clock, the rubbish truck comes. It is loud and the men talk in strong voices. I lie in bed and I listen. At six o'clock, the bread seller pushes a small cart up my street. He shouts one word again and again. People open their windows and they buy hot bread. At seven o'clock, the cars start. I hear engines and footsteps on the pavement. The street is never quiet, but each sound has its own time. I do not need a clock. The street tells me the time. I know my city by its morning music.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: lie in bed and I listen
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: nằm trên giường và lắng nghe
tiếng động cơ và tiếng bước chân

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for daily order
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi mô tả thói quen hàng ngày theo trình tự thời gian, dùng hiện tại đơn: 'I wake up', 'I hear', 'the truck comes', 'the bread seller pushes'. Mỗi câu là một sự việc xảy ra đều đặn.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: When describing a daily order, use present simple: 'I wake up', 'I hear', 'the truck comes', 'the bread seller pushes'. Each clause is a regular event.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Time prepositions 'at' for clock time
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'at' với giờ chính xác: 'at five o'clock', 'at six o'clock', 'at seven o'clock'. Không dùng 'on' hay 'in' cho giờ trong ngày.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'at' with exact clock times: 'at five o'clock', 'at six o'clock', 'at seven o'clock'. Do not use 'on' or 'in' for clock times.

sampleTranslationVi: Tôi sống ở Istanbul, trong một căn hộ nhỏ phía trên một quán trà. Mỗi sáng, tôi thức dậy sớm vì những âm thanh bên ngoài. Âm thanh đầu tiên tôi nghe là tiếng gọi cầu nguyện từ nhà thờ Hồi giáo lớn. Nó rất chậm và đẹp. Sau đó, tôi nghe tiếng chim hải âu. Chúng bay qua mái nhà và kêu vào nhau. Lúc năm giờ, xe rác đến. Nó ồn ào và những người công nhân nói chuyện to. Tôi nằm trên giường và lắng nghe. Lúc sáu giờ, người bán bánh mì đẩy một chiếc xe đẩy nhỏ trên phố. Anh ấy rao một từ liên tục. Mọi người mở cửa sổ và mua bánh mì nóng. Lúc bảy giờ, xe cộ bắt đầu chạy. Tôi nghe tiếng động cơ và tiếng bước chân trên vỉa hè. Đường phố không bao giờ yên tĩnh, nhưng mỗi âm thanh có giờ riêng. Tôi không cần đồng hồ. Đường phố nói cho tôi biết giờ. Tôi biết thành phố của mình qua âm nhạc buổi sáng của nó.

options: In Istanbul, above a tea shop
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I live in Istanbul, in a small flat above a tea shop'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What is the first sound he hears?
options: The call to prayer from the big mosque
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'The first sound I hear is the call to prayer from the big mosque'.

mcQuestions.prompt: When does the rubbish truck come?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'At five o'clock, the rubbish truck comes'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the time and the truck.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does the bread seller do?
options: He pushes a small cart and shouts one word.
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'the bread seller pushes a small cart up my street. He shouts one word again and again'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look at the six o'clock part.

mcQuestions.prompt: Why does the writer not need a clock?
options: Because the street sounds tell him the time.
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I do not need a clock. The street tells me the time'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the sentences near the end.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which preposition is correct: 'I wake up ___ six o'clock'?
mcQuestions.explanation: Use 'at' with clock times: 'at six o'clock'.
mcQuestions.hint: Clock times use one short preposition.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is in the present simple?
options: The truck comes at five o'clock every day.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple with 'every day' shows a habit.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the time phrase 'every day'.

options: I wake up early because of the sounds outside.
I wake up early because the sounds outside.
Because sounds outside I wake up early.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because of' is followed by a noun: 'because of the sounds'.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'because of' you need a noun.

mcQuestions.prompt: What do people do when the bread seller comes?
options: They open their windows and buy hot bread.
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'People open their windows and they buy hot bread'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the action after the seller arrives.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word best describes the street in the morning?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'The street is never quiet'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do the sounds of a city tell us so much about its people and their work?
